buggy_code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import numpy as np

X = np.random.randn(1000, 10)
y = np.random.randn(1000)

scaler = StandardScaler()

# FIT on full dataset, including future data
X_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X)

# Train model on scaled data
model.fit(X_scaled, y)

# Backtest using same scaler
predictions = model.predict(X_scaled)
clean_code:
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import numpy as np

X = np.random.randn(1000, 10)
y = np.random.randn(1000)

# Split into train and test FIRST
train_size = int(0.7 * len(X))
X_train, X_test = X[:train_size], X[train_size:]
y_train, y_test = y[:train_size], y[train_size:]

scaler = StandardScaler()

# FIT ONLY on training data
X_train_scaled = scaler.fit_transform(X_train)

# Train model on scaled training data
model.fit(X_train_scaled, y_train)

# Transform test data using train scaler
X_test_scaled = scaler.transform(X_test)
predictions = model.predict(X_test_scaled)
description: StandardScaler is fit on the entire dataset before training the model.
explanation: When you fit_transform on all X data, the scaler learns the mean and standard deviation from all observations including those that would be in the 'future' during backtesting. This leaks information from out-of-sample data into the scaling, which is then used to train the model. The model learns features that are predictive only because they were scaled with knowledge of future data.
suggested_fix: Always fit the scaler on training data only. Apply the trained scaler to test/validation data separately.

issues.title: Data leakage from scaling entire dataset
